app.title: Solar Panel Thermal Simulator
app.subtitle: Thermal behaviour and energy balance of a single module
app.tagline: Enter the conditions, pick a model, and see where every watt goes.
scenario.title: Scenarios
help.irradiance: Plane-of-array solar irradiance reaching the panel.
help.wind_speed: Wind speed over the panel; higher wind cools it.
help.sky_temp: Effective radiative sky temperature. Leave on 'auto' for T_air − 20 °C.
help.gnd_temp: Ground/surface temperature seen by the back. Leave on 'auto' for T_air.
help.eta_stc: Electrical conversion efficiency at Standard Test Conditions.
help.gamma: Relative power change per °C above 25 °C; typically about −0.004.
help.alpha: Fraction of incident sunlight absorbed by the panel.
help.noct: Nominal Operating Cell Temperature from the datasheet.
help.area: Panel area, used to convert W/m² into absolute watts.
help.mass_cp: Areal heat capacity; only affects the transient warm-up.
help.mounting: How the module is mounted; controls back-side cooling.
desc.reflected: Sunlight not absorbed — reflected or lost optically. Not heat.
desc.electricity: Useful electrical power extracted from the panel.
desc.radiated: Long-wave infrared radiated to the sky and ground.
desc.convected: Heat swept away by air moving over the panel.
desc.conducted: Heat conducted through the frame into the mounting structure.
desc.stored: Net heating rate of the panel; zero once steady state is reached.
note.stored_steady: In steady state nothing is stored — energy in equals energy out. The raised temperature is a consequence, not stored energy.
note.radiated_vs_total: "Radiated to surroundings" is strictly Q_rad. The total handed to the environment as heat is Q_rad + Q_conv + Q_cond.
note.single_panel: For a single panel the warming of the wider surroundings is negligible; we show waste-heat density (W/m²) and panel temperature, not a change in ambient air temperature.
note.model_split: This model gives only the temperature; the three heat pathways are apportioned from the net heat Q_gen by their instantaneous magnitude. Only the Full energy balance solves them self-consistently.
note.closure: Balance closes: the six channels sum to the incident G.
anim.transient_note: Warm-up mode: the panel heats from ambient towards steady state; the flows grow as it warms.
anim.hint: Arrow size, particle rate and colours scale with the computed fluxes.
math.intro: The equation is language-neutral; the explanation is translated.
assume.steady: Steady state: thermal mass is ignored (valid away from sudden changes in sun or cloud).
assume.faiman.1: Empirical fit that lumps radiation and convection into u₀ and u₁.
assume.faiman.2: Back-side radiation is not modelled explicitly.
assume.pvsyst.1: Removes the extracted electrical power from the heat load via the efficiency term.
assume.pvsyst.2: U_c and U_v depend on mounting; the wind term is often set to zero.
assume.sandia.1: Exponential empirical fit (King); a, b and ΔT come from the module and mounting type.
assume.sandia.2: ΔT adds the temperature drop between the cell and the module back surface.
assume.noct.1: Single-point benchmark defined at 800 W/m², 20 °C air and 1 m/s wind.
assume.noct.2: Linear in irradiance; ignores wind speed and efficiency.
assume.balance.1: Solves the surface energy balance explicitly for the cell temperature.
assume.balance.2: Radiation is computed in kelvin; sky and ground temperatures are estimated when left on auto.
assume.balance.3: Back-side exchange is scaled by a mounting factor f_back (1 open, 0 insulated).
assume.balance.4: Transient mode integrates the thermal mass C = m·cp / A over time.
general.run_hint: Set the conditions in the sidebar and press Compute.
